vulnerability.title: Ensure the Default Bash Umask is Set Correctly
vulnerability.description: To ensure the default umask for users of the Bash shell is set properly, add or correct the umask setting in /etc/bashrc to read as follows: umask
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q bash; }; then var_accounts_user_umask='' grep -q "^[^#]*\bumask" /etc/bashrc && \ sed -i -E -e "s/^([^#]*\bumask)[[:space:]]+[[:digit:]]+/\1 $var_accounts_user_umask/g" /etc/bashrc if ! [ $? -eq 0 ]; then echo "umask $var_accounts_user_umask" >> /etc/bashrc fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Setting umask in /etc/bashrc is a standard hardening step that only affects default file permissions for newly created files; it does not modify authentication, networking, or core system files, and poses minimal risk of lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: If umask is set too restrictively, it could cause permission issues for applications or users expecting default permissions.; Incorrect syntax could cause bash to fail to source /etc/bashrc for non-login shells.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for accounts_umask_etc_bashrc (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_accounts_umask_etc_bashrc). Could not verify.
